title: Quantum Circuit Structure Optimization for Quantum Reinforcement Learning
summary: The paper addresses the limitations of classical neural network-based reinforcement learning (RL), such as the curse of dimensionality and sample inefficiency in high-dimensional spaces. It proposes that quantum reinforcement learning (QRL) using quantum neural networks (QNNs) is a potential solution. The core focus is on a critical, unresolved issue in QRL: the suboptimal, empirically-designed structure of the parameterized quantum circuit (PQC). The paper advocates for an automatic optimization of the PQC's gate types, placements, and depths to improve learning stability, convergence speed, and overall performance for specific problems.

problemStatement.classicalRLLimitations: Classical NN-based RL faces the curse of dimensionality and sample inefficiency in high-dimensional state and action spaces, leading to slow convergence and high computational costs.
problemStatement.qrlLimitation: Current QRL research often uses PQC structures designed with empirical intuition or fixed patterns, which are suboptimal for specific problems, restricting learning performance and increasing computational costs.

proposedSolution.coreIdea: To automatically search and select the optimal PQC structure.
proposedSolution.optimizationBasis: Based on environmental characteristics and learning objectives.

quantumNeuralNetwork.description: Consists of three components: an encoder, a parameterized quantum circuit (PQC), and a measurement stage.

components.name: Encoder
components.description: Converts classical inputs into quantum states for processing within the quantum circuit.

components.name: Parameterized Quantum Circuit (PQC)
components.description: Performs linear and nonlinear transformations similar to hidden layers in classical neural networks.

components.name: Measurement
components.description: Converts quantum states into classical outputs for verification.

keyConcepts.parameterizedQuantumCircuit: The core component of a QNN whose structure, gate arrangement, and parameter configuration directly influence learning stability, convergence speed, and the expressiveness of the quantum circuit.
